comments.text: Since this problem is considering the regime of large $x$ and $y$, the falsifiable tag should be replaced by open, no? Any potential counterexample will not be enough.
I think the statement from [HeRi73] should be $$ \pi(x+y) >\pi(x) +\pi(y) +c\frac{y}{(\log y) ^2} $$ for some $c>0$. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
That line cannot be meant, looking at x=y.
Adenwalla is correct (the statement is only for infinitely many $x$ for a given $y$, so one doesn't have to worry about obstructions at $y=x$).
In theory, If we had a lot of time and a lot of computational power available, we could write down explicit counterexamples to this conjecture, but even this would not settle the problem.
